49f0cabf-5c56-4ee6-88f3-76508251f4ea.text:
Measuring Issue Ownership using Word Embeddings ∗

Amaru Cuba Gyllensten RISE AI amaru.cuba.gyllensten@ri.se
Magnus Sahlgren RISE AI magnus.sahlgren@ri.se

Abstract

Sentiment and topic analysis are common methods used for social media monitoring.

1d9499d3-0277-4eeb-bc9d-e48921292988.label: text_chunk
1d9499d3-0277-4eeb-bc9d-e48921292988.text: Essentially, these methods answers questions such as, “what is being talked about, regarding X ”, and “what do people feel, regarding X ”. In this paper, we investigate another venue for social media monitoring, namely issue owner- ship and agenda setting , which are concepts from political science that have been used to explain voter choice and electoral outcomes.

192b0aa1-3f8c-4884-90b6-5ef4190283ee.label: text_chunk
192b0aa1-3f8c-4884-90b6-5ef4190283ee.text: We argue that issue alignment and agenda set- ting can be seen as a kind of semantic source similarity of the kind “how similar is source A to issue owner P , when talking about is- sue X ”, and as such can be measured using word/document embedding techniques. We present work in progress towards measuring that kind of conditioned similarity, and intro- duce a new notion of similarity for predic- tive embeddings.

8b832c8b-7c3b-42b6-8208-04641ec6a45a.label: text_chunk
8b832c8b-7c3b-42b6-8208-04641ec6a45a.text:
We then test this method by measuring the similarity between politi- cally aligned media and political parties, con- ditioned on bloc-specific issues.

1 Introduction

Social Media Monitoring (SMM; i.e. monitor- ing of online discussions in social media) has become an established application domain with a large body of scientific literature, and con- siderable commercial interest. The subfields of Topic Detection and Tracking ( Allan et al.

c09ca8de-f07f-4671-b122-65dfdd888f95.label: text_chunk
c09ca8de-f07f-4671-b122-65dfdd888f95.text:
, 1998 ; Sridhar , 2015 ) and Sentiment Analysis ( Turney , 2002 ; Pang and Lee , 2008 ; Liu , 2012 ; Pozzi et al.

 , 2016 ) are both scientific topics spawned entirely within the SMM domain.

bd6f703e-4051-4d2f-8956-c4044df7d55a.label: text_chunk
bd6f703e-4051-4d2f-8956-c4044df7d55a.text:
In its most basic form, SMM entails nothing more than counting occur- rences of terms in data; producing frequency lists of commonly used vocabulary, and matching of term sets related to various topics and sentiments.More sophisticated approaches use various forms of probabilistic topic detection (such as Latent Dirichlet Allocation) and sentiment analysis based on supervised machine learning.

The central questions SMM seeks to answer are “what do users talk about?” and “how do they feel about it?” .

688ead04-f551-4e01-89be-706f044f3da6.label: text_chunk
688ead04-f551-4e01-89be-706f044f3da6.text:
Answers to these ques- tions may provide useful insight for market re- search and communications departments. It is ap- parent how product and service companies may use such analysis to gain an understanding of their target audience.

 It is also apparent how such analysis may be used in the context of elec- tions for providing an indication of citizens’ opin- ions as manifested in what they write in social media.

9e10e837-0d6d-4164-b293-85e70a6b348a.label: text_chunk
9e10e837-0d6d-4164-b293-85e70a6b348a.text:
There are numerous studies attempting to use various forms of social media monitoring techniques to predict the outcome of elections, with varying success ( Bermingham and Smeaton , 2011 ; Ceron et al. , 2015 ).

Most notably, the recent examples of the inade- quacy of standard opinion measuring techniques to forecast the most recent US election and the Brexit demonstrate that for certain questions re- lated to measuring mass opinion, standard SMM techniques may be inadequate.

76f7039f-a351-4b22-9824-faf0dd9d8f84.label: text_chunk
76f7039f-a351-4b22-9824-faf0dd9d8f84.text:
Political scientists have used the concepts of agenda setting and is- sue ownership to explain voter choice and elec- tion outcomes ( Kl¨uver and naki Sagarzazu , 2016 ; Kiousis et al.

 , 2015 ; Stubager , 2018 ).

51d47921-6ab2-4cfd-abb9-bd17abe62a0c.label: text_chunk
51d47921-6ab2-4cfd-abb9-bd17abe62a0c.text: In short, the issue ownership theory of voting states that vot- ers identify the most credible party proponent of a particular issue and cast their ballots for that is- sue owner ( B´elanger and Meguid , 2008 ). Agenda setting refers to the media’s role in influencing the importance of issues in the public agenda ( Mccombs and Reynolds , 2002 ).

a1f8b409-b19b-4e7f-9500-6f10a019de7a.label: text_chunk
a1f8b409-b19b-4e7f-9500-6f10a019de7a.text:
Note that current social media monitoring techniques are unable to measure these concepts in a satisfactory manner; it does not suffice to measure the occurrence of cer-
∗ This research was supported by the Swedish Research Council under contract 2017-02429
tain keywords, since most parties tend to use the same vocabulary to discuss issues, and sentiment analysis does not touch upon the issue ownership and agenda setting questions.

371f86dc-3ef2-4a9c-96f1-efb730d842eb.label: text_chunk
371f86dc-3ef2-4a9c-96f1-efb730d842eb.text:
What is needed for measuring issue ownership and agenda setting is a way to measure language use , i.

e. when talking about an issue, to which extent does the language used align with issue owner A vs. issue owner B .We argue that issue alignment can be seen as a kind of semantic source similarity of the kind “how similar is source A to issue owner P , when talking about issue X ”, and as such can be measured using word/document embedding tech- niques.

6d2366c4-8fff-4cd8-9849-570b58f8525e.label: text_chunk
6d2366c4-8fff-4cd8-9849-570b58f8525e.text:
To measure that kind of conditioned sim- ilarity we introduce a new notion of similarity for predictive word embeddings.

 This method enables us to manipulate the similarity measure by weight- ing the set of entities we account for in the pre- dictive scoring function. The proposed method is applied to measure similarity between party pro- grams and various subsets of online text sources, conditioned on bloc specific issues. The results indicate that this conditioning disentangles simi- larity.

d540c0b6-e0ff-4d83-9d58-7b20af0f01da.label: text_chunk
d540c0b6-e0ff-4d83-9d58-7b20af0f01da.text:
We can, for example, observe that while the Left Party representation is, overall, similar to that of nativist media, it differs significantly on nativist issue, while this effect is not seen to the same ex- tent on more mainstream left wing or right wing media.

2 Vector Similarity

Vector similarity has been a foundational concept in natural language processing ever sine the intro- duction of the vector space model for information retrieval by Salton ( 1971 ).

d8c64c3e-a5ff-449e-88a1-4b1ba6091db0.label: text_chunk
d8c64c3e-a5ff-449e-88a1-4b1ba6091db0.text: In this model, queries and document are represented as vectors in term space, and similarity is expressed using cosine similarity.

a8694c12-04fb-442a-b347-95d5bc23f11c.label: text_chunk
a8694c12-04fb-442a-b347-95d5bc23f11c.text: The main reason for using cosine sim- ilarity in the vector space model is that it normal- izes for vector length; the fact that a document (or query) contains a certain word is more important than how many times it occurs in the document.The vector space model was the main source of inspiration for early work on vector se- mantics , such as Latent Semantic Analysis ( Deerwester et al.

891e5c55-0b53-4fa0-ace1-3924380718d5.label: text_chunk
891e5c55-0b53-4fa0-ace1-3924380718d5.text:
, 1990 ; Landauer and Dumais , 1997 ) and the works on word space models by Sch¨utze ( 1992 , 1993 ). These works contin- ued to embrace cosine similarity as the simi- larity metric of choice, since length normaliza- tion is equally desired when words are repre-
sented by vectors whose elements encode (some function of) co-occurrences with other words.

a3db173d-669f-47bf-b03b-24d56feceb96.label: text_chunk
a3db173d-669f-47bf-b03b-24d56feceb96.text: Contemporary research on distributional seman- tics ( Sahlgren , 2006 ; Bullinaria and Levy , 2007 ; Turney and Pantel , 2010 ; Pennington et al. , 2014 ) still use largely the same mathematical machin- ery as the vector space model, and cosine sim- ilarity is still the preferred similarity metric due to its simplicity and use of length normaliza- tion.

cae63e79-03e8-4822-8bb0-9ad10c6fdbb1.label: text_chunk
cae63e79-03e8-4822-8bb0-9ad10c6fdbb1.text: Even neural language models, which originate from the neural network community, employ cosine similarity to quantify similarity between learned representations ( Mikolov et al. , 2013 ; Bojanowski et al. , 2017 ).Word embeddings , as these techniques are nowadays referred to, have been used ex- tensively in SMM, both for topic detection ( Sridhar , 2015 ) and for sentiment analysis ( Severyn and Moschitti , 2015 ).

0fe2a2da-5ce5-497b-b770-1d72a34234bf.label: text_chunk
0fe2a2da-5ce5-497b-b770-1d72a34234bf.text:
To the best of our knowledge, only one previous study ( Dahlberg and Sahlgren , 2014 ) has used word em- beddings to analyze issue ownership. However, that study relied on simple nearest neighbor anal- ysis using cosine similarity to study language use in the Swedish blogosphere.We believe that prediction-based word embed- dings such as Word2Vec are amenable to another notion of similarity, which we call predictive sim- ilarity.

3 Experiments

To answer the language similarity question posed by issue ownership we measure aggregate predic- tive similarity between party platforms and var- ious subsets of online text data, conditioned on words pertaining to left wing issues, right wing is- sues, nativist issues, and general political topics.

For the data crawled by Trendiction, we take the source to be the domain name of the document, e.g. www.wikipedia.

4 Discussion

As can be seen in Table 3 , there is a marked dif- ference when conditioning on issues versus using regular document — i.e. cosine — similarity. Fur- thermore, we observe that conditioned similarity seems to align left wing media with left wing par- ties, nativist media with the Swedish Democrats, but not align right wing media with right wing par- ties.

The results of this normalization, i.e. the residuals, can be observed in Table 4 .

5 Conclusion

In this paper we have introduced some very pre- liminary results on how to measure similarities in language use, conditioned on discourse, e.g. “how similar is The BBC to The Daily Mail , when talk- ing about Climate Change ”.

234ac3b9-8c3b-4a8e-aad2-366aef41bd0c.label: text_chunk
234ac3b9-8c3b-4a8e-aad2-366aef41bd0c.text: The end goal is to measure aggregate similarity in specific issues, an- swering questions such as “when talking about health policy , to which extent does the general lan- guage use align with Source A , Source B , etc.

b876d952-2641-46b6-bdbf-beea7681e6d0.label: text_chunk
b876d952-2641-46b6-bdbf-beea7681e6d0.text: ”, and use such an aggregate measure to study issue own- ership at scale.We believe that issue ownership and agenda set- ting can be explored through the lens of language use and similarity, but deem it necessary to con- dition similarity to the specific issue at hand. The reason for this is the need to distinguish between level of engagement in an issue and agreement in an issue: two sources that talk a lot about an issue — e.g.

f80634d3-213e-4337-92b2-21e4318992e1.label: text_chunk
f80634d3-213e-4337-92b2-21e4318992e1.text: health insurance — but in very different ways should not be considered similar. Dually, if a source very rarely talks about an issue, but consistently does so in a way that is very similar to the way some political party talks about it, we consider it reasonable to believe that that source’s opinion aligns with the political party in question on that specific issue.

f623f15d-8457-430d-a321-2c43f4a13354.label: text_chunk
f623f15d-8457-430d-a321-2c43f4a13354.text:
While we have not found a satisfactory, direct, evaluation of this task, we do believe that the ex- amples we put forward show some face validity of the proposed method at measuring ideological alignment.

1. **Foundational Publications**:
   - **Natural OWL** is based on the principles outlined in **Reiter and Dale 2000**, which discusses the processing stages of Natural Language Generation (NLG) systems.
   - The processing stages and linguistic resources of **Natural OWL** are influenced by findings from **Mellish et al. 2006**, detailing typical characteristics of NLG systems.

2. **Integration and Functionality**:
   - **Natural OWL** is a plug-in for **Protégé**, enhancing its capabilities in ontology management.
   - Within its framework, **Natural OWL** processes identifiers like 'exhibit 49' to classify and refer to items without using their natural language names.
   - It assigns importance scores to message triples based on their relations, indicating its role in evaluating the significance of information.

3. **Model Application**:
   - The **Natural OWL** framework applies aggregation rules to outputs generated by the **ILP Model**, showcasing its utilization of model results for enhanced processing.

4. **Evolution of Systems**:
   - **ILPNLG** is a modified version of **Natural OWL**, indicating that it builds upon the foundational concepts established by Natural OWL.

5. **System Components**:
   - A key component of the **Natural OWL** system is the **PIPELINE**, which is responsible for handling natural language name selection.

This summary encapsulates the critical relationships and functionalities of Natural OWL, highlighting its theoretical foundations, practical applications, and evolutionary context within the realm of NLG systems.

The relationships involving the entity ILPNLG highlight its role in natural language generation and its interactions with various extensions, comparisons, and challenges. Here’s a summary of the key relationships:

1. **ILPNLG and ILPNLGEXTEND**: ILPNLG serves as the foundational algorithm for ILPNLGEXTEND, which is an extension designed to enhance ILPNLG's capabilities, particularly in managing multiple alternative natural language names.

2. **Performance Challenges**: ILPNLG struggles with processing the Consumer Electronics Ontology, noted for its complexity and data volume, which results in slower performance.

3. **Approximation and Efficiency**: ILPNLGAPPROX is developed as an approximation of ILPNLG to improve efficiency in text generation, indicating a relationship where ILPNLG is both approximated by and serves as a basis for this new algorithm.

4. **Comparative Analysis**: ILPNLG is compared to pipeline systems regarding the fluency and naturalness of the generated texts, where it is found to produce more compact texts than these systems.

5. **Domain Context**: ILPNLG operates within the context of the Disease Ontology, which affects its performance due to the varying lengths of natural language names it processes.

6. **Reporting Functionality**: ILPNLG generates descriptive texts about wine, showcasing its application in reporting on specific attributes.

7. **Competitive Landscape**: ILPNLG competes with the NESHORT algorithm, with both being evaluated for their performance on the Consumer Electronics ontology.

Overall, ILPNLG is a central entity in natural language generation, with extensions and approximations aimed at enhancing its functionality and efficiency, while also facing challenges in specific domains and competing with other algorithms.

5. **ILP Model and Facts**: The model utilizes facts as input data, which are critical for its optimization processes and for generating meaningful results.
